steps.title: Identify the starting value and growth
steps.content: The problem gives the first point directly: when $x=0$, $y=80$. A $15\%$ increase means the next value is $115\%$ of the old value, so each step multiplies by $1.15$.

steps.title: Find the exponential pattern
steps.content: An exponential model has the form $y=a(b)^x$. The value at $x=0$ is $a$, so $a=80$. A $15\%$ increase means the growth factor is $1.15$, so the model is $y=80(1.15)^x$.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The matching choice has the starting value $80$ and the growth factor $1.15$. The other choices either use the wrong starting value or the wrong multiplier, so A is correct.
